title: Token receiver fallback handling ERC721/ERC1155 callbacks via inline selector dispatch
explanation: Three independent token receiver functions are collapsed into a single fallback dispatcher. (1) shr(224, calldataload(0)) extracts msg.sig as a uint256 in one shift. (2) An or-chain of eq() compares against the three known selectors in a branchless pattern. (3) On match, the selector is stored at 0x20 and returned from offset 0x3c — the last 4 bytes of the 32-byte word are the right-padded bytes4 return value. (4) Unknown selectors fall through to the FnSelectorNotRecognized() revert with the standard 4-byte selector at offset 0x1c. Eliminates the function table and dispatch jumps the compiler would otherwise generate.
when_to_apply: Smart accounts and vaults that must accept ERC721 / ERC1155 transfers without per-callback dispatch overhead
when_not_to_apply: Contracts that need to actually process receipt callbacks (e.g. minting derivative tokens, updating internal accounting based on the received id/amount)
